name: Conductor

form_factor.comment: Native macOS (Apple Silicon) desktop app that orchestrates Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor agents in parallel. Per-form-factor links: Mac App: https://www.conductor.build/.

released_in.comment: Launched publicly by Melty Labs in July 2025 (Show HN) as a Mac app for running parallel Claude Code agents.

latest_major_update.comment: v0.72.0 (July 3, 2026) improves the workspace experience with better error handling and fixes across scripts, comments, archiving, and notifications.

pricing.value: Free app; bring your own Claude/Codex/Cursor subscription or API key
pricing.comment: The app itself is free; you supply your own Claude Pro/Max or Codex/Cursor subscription, or an API key, per the harness settings.

some_free_usage.comment: App is free to download, but real agent work requires a paid Claude/Codex/Cursor subscription or API key.

no_account_required.comment: Requires signing into an underlying agent (Claude Code, Codex, or Cursor) account or supplying an API key.

can_use_other_subscriptions.comment: Uses whatever you are already logged into for Claude Code/Codex (Claude Pro/Max or API key), or an API key set in Settings → Harnesses/Environment.

rules.comment: Reads repository guidance (CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md) via the underlying agent, plus repo-scoped Conductor settings.

skills.comment: Exposes the underlying agent's Skills within a workspace.

hooks.comment: Project scripts and checks run around agent execution; underlying Claude Code/Codex lifecycle hooks also apply, but Conductor adds no separate hook system.

mcp_servers.comment: Conductor sessions can use MCP servers where the selected agent host supports MCP; Claude Code and Codex load their agent-specific MCP configuration.

custom_commands.comment: Slash commands of the underlying agent (e.g. Codex /goal) are available inside a Conductor workspace; no separate Conductor command system.

goal_command.comment: Codex goals (/goal) are surfaced in Conductor for Codex sessions, keeping objectives active across turns.

subagents.comment: Orchestrates multiple agents in parallel isolated workspaces rather than nested subagents within one session.

monitor.comment: Dashboard shows what every agent is doing and where it is stuck; sidebar badges flag workspaces where agents finished or need input.

model_selection.comment: Model picker lets you choose a Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, or OpenCode model per workspace.

approval_mode.comment: Plan Mode requires approval before implementation; Fast Mode skips planning for quick edits.

sandbox_mode.comment: Each workspace is an isolated git worktree; process/network sandboxing depends on the underlying agent.

resume.comment: Workspaces persist so you can return to an in-progress agent session; no session-id resume flag exposed.

non_interactive.comment: GUI Mac app; no headless/print run mode.

transcripts.comment: Keeps per-workspace conversation and diff history in-app; underlying agent transcripts also persist on disk.

git_integration.comment: Deep git integration: worktrees, branch management, inline diff review with comments the agent responds to before pushing.

hosted_agent.comment: Agents run locally on your Mac; no managed cloud execution platform.

custom_model_provider.comment: Limited to the supported harnesses (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode) and their model providers, not arbitrary OpenAI-compatible endpoints.

git_worktrees.comment: Core model: Conductor creates each workspace as a separate git worktree (1 workspace = 1 branch = 1 working tree), so parallel agents never conflict.

chat_forking.comment: One workspace maps to one branch; no documented way to fork a conversation into a new branch/session.

done_notifications.comment: Sidebar badges tell you which workspaces need attention when their agents finish or need your input.

byok_prompt_caching.comment: Runs the real Claude Code / Codex CLIs on your own subscription or key, so their native Anthropic/OpenAI prompt caching still applies.

notes: Mac-only (Apple Silicon) orchestrator for running parallel Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor agents in isolated git worktrees, with inline diff review before pushing.
